encounter.type: Office visit — new patient consultation (initial electrophysiology consultation)
encounter.reason: Initial EP consultation for atrial fibrillation; evaluation of catheter ablation candidacy
encounter.service_provider: University of Minnesota Cardiac Electrophysiology Clinic
encounter.location: University of Minnesota Cardiac Electrophysiology Clinic, Minneapolis, MN

conditions.name: atrial fibrillation, likely persistent
conditions.note: Refined characterization at EP consultation: AF classified as likely persistent based on continuous AF on ECG and approximately 3 months of ongoing symptoms. EHRA symptom class IIb (symptoms affecting daily activity). Ablation candidacy assessment initiated.

document_references.description: Comprehensive electrophysiology consultation documenting referral from Dr. Angela Whitfield at Minnehaha Internal Medicine, detailed cardiac history including symptom onset in November 2025, review of outside records (ECG confirming AF, echocardiogram showing mild LA dilation and preserved LVEF, normal labs), physical examination confirming AF with controlled rate, assessment characterizing AF as likely persistent with EHRA class IIb symptoms, discussion of catheter ablation candidacy with patient and wife, and order for 48-hour Holter monitor to quantify AF burden.
